name: Horne v. Flores
question: Did the lower courts err in their analysis under Rule 60(b)(5) regarding Arizona's contention that changes in education law, including increased state funding, changes in the management of the school district involved, and passage of the No Child Left Behind Act had so altered the foundations of prior court rulings that relief from such judgments was warranted?
description: A case in which the Court found that the lower court incorrectly analyzed Rule 60(b)(5), which allows a party to seek relief from a judgment if a significant change in the law has occurred.
